text:
maxTimeMS accounts for server RTT
When constructing a command, drivers use the timeouts option to derive a value for the maxTimeMs command option and the socket
timeout. The full time to round trip a command is (network RTT + server-side execution time). If both maxTimeMs and socket timeout were
set to the same value, the server would never be able to respond with a MaxTimeMSExpired error because drivers would hit the socket
timeout first and close the connection. This would lead to connection churn if the specified timeout is too low. To allow the server to
gracefully error and avoid churn, drivers must account for the network round trip in the maxTimeMs calculation.
